items.check: The output is a finished public course or workshop promotional poster, not a notes page, extraction table, screenshot of source material, or generic summary.
items.scoring_hint: Full credit for a self-contained poster with promotional hierarchy; partial credit for a poster-like image that is incomplete or too document-like. Task completion should drop clearly when required env-grounded content is largely missing, even if the image is visually polished. Placeholder language such as coming soon, unavailable, pending, details will be available, cannot finalize, or not accessible does not count as completion.

items.check: The poster attempts to include only the required display content named in task.md: final public title, lead instructor, approved schedule, target audience, and exactly three learning highlights.
items.scoring_hint: Do not require identical wording for highlights, but the output should clearly cover all requested content types. Do not penalize CTA omission. Task completion should drop clearly when required env-grounded content is largely missing, even if the image is visually polished. Placeholder language such as coming soon, unavailable, pending, details will be available, cannot finalize, or not accessible does not count as completion.

items.check: Uses the final public title, Climate Data Stories for Civic Action, rather than a draft or internal title.
items.scoring_hint: Minor punctuation or adding 'studio' is acceptable; replacing it with a draft title is not.

items.check: Identifies Dr. Maya Chen as the lead instructor and does not promote a guest contributor as the lead.
items.scoring_hint: Maya Chen without the honorific is acceptable.

items.check: Uses the approved public schedule: Wednesdays, May 6-27, 2026, 6:00-8:15 PM.
items.scoring_hint: Accept listing all four Wednesday dates if the time is correct; do not accept replaced April dates or tentative language.

items.check: Names the target audience as city staff, nonprofit analysts, and community organizers working with climate or resilience data.
items.scoring_hint: Close paraphrases are fine if all three audience groups remain recognizable.

items.check: Includes exactly three poster-safe highlights grounded in the final outcomes: public narratives from messy climate data, accessible charts/map captions, and a one-page action brief.
items.scoring_hint: Do not penalize concise paraphrase; penalize adding extra highlights or choosing internal/process-only notes as highlights.

items.check: Avoids draft, internal, unconfirmed, or unsupported details such as old titles, old dates, venue TBA, tuition, certificate claims, registration cap, internal sponsor, draft affiliation, or waitlist notes.
items.scoring_hint: This is negative-only: award full credit when these details are absent. Penalize only explicit inclusion of prohibited details.

items.check: No call to action is required. If no CTA appears, award full credit; if a CTA appears, it must use the public apply deadline and URL rather than waitlist, internal registration, a wrong URL, a wrong deadline, or unsupported details.
items.scoring_hint: Omitting the CTA earns full credit. Deduct only for an included CTA that is wrong, internal, unsupported, or misleading.

items.check: Makes the course title and three learning highlights the main visual message, with schedule, audience, and instructor as clear supporting information.
items.scoring_hint: The viewer should understand what the course is and why to attend before reading small details. Structuring credit requires mostly correct core env facts; placeholder or generic content should score low even with a tidy layout.

items.check: Compresses the long brief into concise public-facing poster copy rather than copying long paragraphs, FAQ text, internal notes, or module tables.
items.scoring_hint: Reward selective synthesis and readable text density. Structuring credit requires mostly correct core env facts; placeholder or generic content should score low even with a tidy layout.

items.check: Looks like a polished course/workshop poster with deliberate typography, spacing, color, and composition rather than a plain document page or placeholder template.
items.scoring_hint: Style can vary; focus on professional poster communication. Score only visual clarity, hierarchy, readability, composition, polish, and platform fit here; do not use this visual item to compensate for missing/incorrect env facts or to penalize forbidden facts.

items.check: All essential text is readable, non-overlapping, and has sufficient contrast at normal viewing size.
items.scoring_hint: Minor small-print issues are acceptable; core facts must remain legible. Score only visual clarity, hierarchy, readability, composition, polish, and platform fit here; do not use this visual item to compensate for missing/incorrect env facts or to penalize forbidden facts.

items.check: Information density is appropriate for a poster: not overcrowded, not sparse, no obvious template placeholders or irrelevant filler.
items.scoring_hint: Reward clean hierarchy and visual breathing room. Score only visual clarity, hierarchy, readability, composition, polish, and platform fit here; do not use this visual item to compensate for missing/incorrect env facts or to penalize forbidden facts.

score_caps.reason: The output does not produce a usable promotional poster image.
Core facts are broadly wrong, such as using the wrong title, wrong schedule, and wrong lead instructor.
The poster explicitly includes multiple forbidden or unsupported details.
Most core poster text is unreadable or visually incoherent.
